Files: src/graph_executor.py
Parent: bees-7b8n

Acceptance: GraphExecutor.traverse() correctly returns related ticket IDs for all four relationship types using only in-memory data.

title: Add auto-fix functionality to linter for common violations
description:
Context: sanitize_hive() needs to automatically fix detected problems where possible, not just report them.

What to Modify:

  - Orphaned parent/child relationships: Add missing bidirectional links
  - Orphaned dependencies: Add missing bidirectional dependency links
  - Missing required fields: Add with sensible defaults
- Track fixes in LinterReport with new 'fixes' field listing what was changed
- Save modified tickets back to disk using write_ticket_file()
- Cannot auto-fix: invalid IDs, cycles, cross-hive violations when disallowed
- If any violations cannot be fixed, return report with remaining errors

Why: sanitize_hive() needs to fix malformed tickets automatically. Extending linter is cleaner than duplicating validation logic.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Linter auto-fixes bidirectional relationship mismatches
- LinterReport includes list of fixes applied
- Modified tickets are written back to filesystem

title: Add bidirectional relationship updates when creating tickets
description:
Context: When creating a ticket with parent or dependencies, must update related tickets to maintain bidirectional consistency (parent's children array, dependents' up/down_dependencies arrays).

Requirements:
- When creating ticket with parent field, read parent ticket and add new ticket ID to parent's children array
- When creating ticket with up_dependencies, read each blocking ticket and add new ticket ID to its down_dependencies array
- When creating ticket with down_dependencies, read each blocked ticket and add new ticket ID to its up_dependencies array
- Use src/reader.py to read related tickets
- Use src/writer.py to update related ticket files
- Handle case where related ticket doesn't exist (raise error)
- Update all related tickets atomically

References: Parent Task bees-7df, bidirectional consistency requirement from Epic bees-gr5
Files: src/mcp_server.py (relationship sync logic in create_ticket handler)

Acceptance: Creating ticket with relationships automatically updates all related tickets bidirectionally.

title: Fix HTTP server shutdown and error handling
description: Critical issues in HTTP server lifecycle management. Problems: (1) Signal handlers call sys.exit(0) immediately after stop_server(), not giving uvicorn time to shut down gracefully. (2) No specific exception handling for HTTP errors (port in use, permission denied, invalid bind address). (3) Signal handlers set up before server initialization, risking shutdown callback on uninitialized server. Solution: Coordinate uvicorn shutdown properly, add HTTP-specific error handling with clear messages, set up signal handlers after successful initialization.

title: Task: Parse hive from ticket_id in update_ticket()
description:
Context: Currently, update_ticket() doesn't know which hive a ticket belongs to. With prefixed IDs, the hive can be inferred from the ticket_id.

What Needs to Change:
- Add ID parsing logic to extract hive prefix from ticket_id
- Split ticket_id on first dot: backend.bees-abc1 → backend
- Route update operation to correct hive based on parsed prefix
- Return error if hive prefix not found in config
- Update src/mcp_server.py update_ticket handler

Why: Self-routing ticket IDs eliminate need for explicit hive parameter in update operations, simplifying the API.

Acceptance Criteria:
- update_ticket() successfully updates tickets using only ticket_id
- System correctly routes to appropriate hive from ID prefix
- Returns clear error for malformed IDs or unknown hives

Epic: bees-bjvt

title: Implement batch relationship update function

- Batch function loads all affected tickets once, applies all changes, then writes all modified tickets
- Reduces file I/O overhead when multiple relationships need updating
- Maintain transaction-like semantics: if any update fails, none are applied

References: Parent Task bees-r10
Files: src/relationship_sync.py

Acceptance: Batch function efficiently handles multiple relationship updates with proper error handling.

title: Task: Implement rename_hive() MCP command
description:
Context: Users may want to rename hives (e.g., "backend" → "api_layer"). This requires updating IDs, filenames, frontmatter, and all references across ALL hives.

What Needs to Change:
- Add rename_hive(old_name: str, new_name: str) MCP tool in src/mcp_server.py
- Normalize both names and validate new_name uniqueness
- Update config: change hive key from old to new, update display_name
- Regenerate all ticket IDs: old_name.bees-* → new_name.bees-*
- Update all filenames in hive directory
- Update ticket frontmatter (id field)
- Scan ALL hives and update cross-references (dependencies, parent, children)
- Update .hive marker file with new display name
- Run linter after rename, mark db corrupt if linter fails

Why: Hives.md specifies this as a supported operation. Must handle cross-hive references even when cross_hive_dependencies is disabled (catches legacy references).

Acceptance Criteria:
- rename_hive() updates config with new normalized/display names
- All ticket IDs regenerated with new prefix
- All filenames updated (old_name.bees-abc1.md → new_name.bees-abc1.md)
- All references updated across all hives (dependencies, parent, children)
- .hive marker updated
- Linter runs after rename, db marked corrupt if fails
- Returns error if old_name doesn't exist or new_name conflicts

Epic: bees-2amw

title: Task: Pipeline Evaluator
description:
Implement the main pipeline evaluator that loads all tickets into memory once, then executes stages sequentially against in-memory data.

Implementation:
- On initialization: Load all tickets from disk into memory (dict of ticket_id -> ticket data)
- Parse YAML frontmatter for all tickets once
- Cache in-memory for duration of query execution
- Execute stages in order against in-memory data
- Pass results from stage N to stage N+1
- Deduplicate ticket IDs after each stage
- Short-circuit if any stage returns empty set
- Determine stage type (search vs graph) and route to appropriate executor
- Support batch query execution: Reuse in-memory data for multiple sequential queries

Files: src/pipeline.py, tests/test_pipeline.py
Epic: bees-2du

Acceptance: Pipeline executes all PRD example queries correctly with single disk load, producing expected filtered ticket lists.

title: Implement sanitize_hive() MCP tool in src/mcp_server.py

- Load .bees/config.json to check allow_cross_hive_dependencies setting
- Scan all tickets in hive directory (not subdirs, only root)
- Instantiate Linter with hive_name, config, and auto_fix=True
- Run linter.run() to validate and fix
- If fixes applied, generate report of changes made
- If linter returns errors after fixes, call mark_corrupt() from corruption_state
- Return detailed dict with: success status, fixes_applied list, errors_remaining list, corruption status

Why: This is the main MCP command users call to fix malformed tickets in a hive.

Acceptance Criteria:
- sanitize_hive() normalizes hive_name and validates it's registered
- Runs linter with hive-aware validations and auto-fix
- Returns error if hive unregistered
- Marks db corrupt if unfixable errors remain
- Returns detailed report of problems found and fixes applied

title: Extend linter to validate cross-hive dependencies against config
description:
Context: When allow_cross_hive_dependencies=false in config, the linter must detect and report tickets that have dependencies/parent/children in other hives.

- Add validate_cross_hive_dependencies() method that:
  - Parses hive prefix from ticket IDs (extract before '.bees-')
  - For each ticket, checks if parent, children, up_dependencies, down_dependencies reference other hives
  - If config disallows cross-hive deps and violations found, adds error with error_type='cross_hive_dependency'
- Call validate_cross_hive_dependencies() from validate_ticket() when config provided

Why: Per hives.md, config option controls whether tickets can reference tickets in other hives. Linter must enforce this.

Acceptance Criteria:
- When allow_cross_hive_dependencies=false, linter detects cross-hive references in parent/children/dependencies
- Generates validation errors with clear messages identifying the cross-hive reference
- When allow_cross_hive_dependencies=true or unset, validation is skipped

title: Fix signal handler to wait for uvicorn shutdown
description: Problem: Signal handlers call sys.exit(0) immediately after stop_server(), not giving uvicorn time to shut down gracefully. The HTTP server runs in uvicorn.run() which is a blocking call. Solution: In src/main.py signal_handler function (lines 40-43), remove sys.exit(0) and instead return/exit cleanly so uvicorn can finish its shutdown. Uvicorn has built-in signal handling - we should rely on it rather than forcing immediate exit. Context: Task bees-kf30, Epic bees-id75. Files: src/main.py. Acceptance: Signal handlers do not call sys.exit(0), uvicorn completes graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
